code: @Manual{,   title = {lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group},   author = {Alexandre Bohyn},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://l-biostat.github.io/lbstproj/}, }

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: lbstproj R package L-Biostat group initializes manages standardized statistical analysis projects. creates project scaffolding, manages “Table Tables” (TOT) metadata tracking system, provides save/load helpers outputs, renders reports.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
code: # Install dependencies devtools::install_deps()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-save.R")  # Run a single named test testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-save.R", filter = "save_figure")  # Check the package (includes tests, examples, vignettes) devtools::check()  # Rebuild documentation from roxygen2 devtools::document()  # Build pkgdown site locally pkgdown::build_site()

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: package organized around project lifecycle: Initialize — create_project() scaffolds new project directory fixed folder structure, DESCRIPTION file, table_of_tables.xlsx (TOT). Create scripts — create_file() generates R scripts Mustache templates inst/templates/. create_from_tot() auto-generates scripts entries TOT. Save outputs — save_figure(), save_table(), save_data() write outputs standardized paths (output/figures/, output/tables/, data/processed/). Run scripts — run_all(), run_all_figures(), run_all_tables() execute scripts relevant R/ subdirectory. Report — create_report() generates Quarto report template; create_chunk() inserts TOT-linked code chunks; run_report() renders . Table Tables (TOT): Excel file (data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx) central metadata registry. tracks every figure table id, type, name, caption. TOT loaded/validated via tot.R queried via get_info.R. Test helpers helper-project.R create minimal -memory TOT fixtures. Template rendering: inst/templates/ contains Mustache templates (.R .qmd). rendered using whisker package variables like {{name}}, {{author}}, {{date}}, {{caption}}, {{path}}. Project root detection: Functions rely usethis/rprojroot conventions — .Rproj file DESCRIPTION project root signals project boundary.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Every R source file begins ASCII art header block:
code: # ├────────────┐ # │  lbstproj  │ # └────────────┘ # filename.R: Short description of what this file contains

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Roxygen2 Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) — docs use markdown. Inline formatting uses cli-style markup: .arg arguments, .val values, .file paths, .fn function names, .strong emphasis. Every exported function @param, @return, @examples.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Use cli::cli_abort() errors context-rich messages. Use cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_info(), cli::cli_alert_warning() user feedback. Respect quiet parameter (package option lbstproj.quiet) suppress output.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Tests use testthat edition 3. Test helpers (tests/testthat/helper-*.R) define local_*() functions create isolated temporary projects clean via withr::local_dir() usethis::local_project(). Use local_lbstproj_project(with_tot = TRUE) get full temp project TOT fixture. Tests create temporary file system state tempdir() — never write package source tree tests.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: functions: snake_case. Parameters follow conventions surrounding codebase (path, name, id, overwrite, quiet, open). Internal (non-exported) helpers documented @keywords internal kept file primary exported function utils.R / utils_path.R / checks.R.

what: Adding new file types or templates
title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Add new template inst/templates/ using Mustache syntax. Add new type create_file() dispatch document . Add corresponding run_all_<type>() wrapper run_all.R scripts type bulk-runnable.

what: Create an R file from a template — create_file
title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: Create R file template. Dedicated templates used type "data", "figure", "table". type uses default template. Files created R/<type>(s)/ (e.g. R/figures/ type = "figure"), except type = "data" goes R/data/, type = "analysis" goes R/analysis/. target directory exist, created automatically.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
code: create_file(   type,   name,   open = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: type Character. Used choose subdirectory template. name Character. File name (without .R). open Logical. Open file creation. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Important messages (e.g. directory creation errors) still shown. Default: TRUE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. ... Additional fields passed data file template.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: Invisibly returns path file created.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Hypothetical project layout: # . # └─ R/ #    ├─ figures/ #    └─ tables/  # 1) Create a figure file in `R/figures/` create_file(type = "figure", name = "hr_by_age") # > ✓ Figure file created at 'R/figures/hr_by_age.R'  # 2) Create a table file in `R/tables/` create_file(type = "table", name = "baseline_characteristics.R") # > ✓ Table file created at 'R/tables/baseline_characteristics.R'  # 3) Create an analysis file (uses default template) in R/analysis/ # > ℹ Created directory 'R/analysis'. # > ✓ Analysis file created at 'R/analysis/primary_model.R'.  # 4) Calling again does not overwrite create_file(type = "analysis", name = "primary_model") # > ! File 'R/analysis/primary_model.R' already exists and will not be overwritten. } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Hypothetical project structure: # R/ # ├── figures/ # │   └── fig_01_flowchart.R # └── tables/ #     └── tab_01_baseline.R  # TOT contains: #   - fig_01_flowchart #   - fig_02_primary #   - tab_01_baseline #   - tab_02_primary  # Dry run (no files created) create_from_tot()  # Example CLI output: # # Figures # ℹ Figures: 2 in TOT, 1 on disk, 1 matched. # ℹ Figures: would generate 1 missing program (keeping 1 existing). # ℹ Figures: would create 1 program in R/figures. # • R/figures/fig_02_primary.R # # Tables # ℹ Tables: 2 in TOT, 1 on disk, 1 matched. # ℹ Tables: would generate 1 missing program (keeping 1 existing). # ℹ Tables: would create 1 program in R/tables. # • R/tables/tab_02_primary.R  # Actual creation create_from_tot(dry_run = FALSE) } # }

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: create_project() initializes new project standardized structure specified path. prompts user essential metadata provided. function creates necessary directories, DESCRIPTION file, table tables (TOT) Excel file. project successfully created, opens new project RStudio.

title: Create a New Project — create_project
code: create_project(   path = ".",   title = NULL,   client = NULL,   department = NULL,   author = NULL,   email = NULL,   version = "1.0.0",   open = rlang::is_interactive(),   force = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: path Path project created (default current directory). title Title project. client Name client. department Department code name. author Author name (first last). version Project version (default 1.0.0). open TRUE, opens new project RStudio (default TRUE interactive session). force TRUE, skips confirmation prompt creating project (default FALSE). quiet TRUE, suppresses informational messages project creation (default FALSE).

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: Invisibly returns active project path.

what: lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group — lbstproj-package
title: lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group — lbstproj-package

what: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: run_all_files() sources R scripts matching glob pattern inside subdirectory project's R/ folder. target subdirectory derived type argument using convention create_file(): "figure" maps R/figures/, "table" maps R/tables/, "data" maps R/data/, "analysis" maps R/analysis/, value maps R/<type>s/. type values "figure" "table", function additionally checks discrepancies files directory table tables (ToT) sourcing, issues warning found. run_all_figures() run_all_tables() convenient wrappers around run_all_files().

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
code: run_all_files(type, glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))  run_all_figures(glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))  run_all_tables(glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: type Character. type scripts run. Controls subdirectory R/ searched: "figure" → R/figures/ "table" → R/tables/ "data" → R/data/ "analysis" → R/analysis/ value → R/<type>s/ glob Character. glob pattern passed fs::dir_ls() include exclude files. Default: "*.R" — matches R scripts (case-sensitive). quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default can changed via options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE).

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: Invisibly returns character vector sourced file paths.

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: run_all_figures(): Run R scripts R/figures run_all_tables(): Run R scripts R/tables

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ run_all_files("figure") run_all_figures()                                 # equivalent to the above run_all_files("data") run_all_files("figure", glob = "fig-[0-9]*.R")   # only numbered figures run_all_files("figure", quiet = TRUE)             # suppress all messages } # }

Render a Rmd/qmd report — run_report
text: file name report file render. Default "report.qmd". file found, function looks file name .Rmd extension throwing error. ... Additional arguments passed rendering function. R Markdown files, passed rmarkdown::render(). Quarto files, passed quarto::quarto_render().

title: Render a Rmd/qmd report — run_report
text: report files render file name. file already exists, overwritten without warning. want keep copy rendered report, use archive_report() function rendering move results/reports/ directory unique name.

title: Render a Rmd/qmd report — run_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default usage run_report() # Rendering a specific R Markdown file to pdf run_report(file = "pdf_report.Rmd", output_format = "pdf_document") } # }

what: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: functions standardize outputs saved lbstproj project: save_data() saves data frame rds data/processed. save_table() saves table rds data/tables optionally export docx results/tables. save_figure() saves figure png (another extension) results/figures. save another R object (e.g. model, mice object, ...) file, need use base::saveRDS manually define file path. function saves object using given name, validating . Names can contain letters, numbers, hyphens.

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
code: save_data(   data,   name,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE) )  save_table(   table,   name,   export = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )  save_figure(   figure,   name,   extension = "png",   width = 6,   height = 4,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: data data.frame save. name Character. File name without extension. overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing file name. FALSE, error thrown target file already exists. Default: TRUE. Outputs overwritten default. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default option can changed using options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE) table gt_tbl object save. export Logical. TRUE, also export table .docx file. Default: TRUE. Word tables always produced unless specified otherwise. ... Additional arguments passed saving function: save_table(): passed gt::gtsave save_figure(): passed ggplot2::ggsave figure ggplot object save. extension Character. Output format figures: "png" "pdf". Default: "png" width, height Numeric. Width height saved figure inches. Default: width 6 height 4. fits nicely portrait A4 page.

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: Invisibly returns path saved file.
